concerns.text: Task T010 (Contract test) is listed BEFORE Task T013 (Implementation of validate_arch.py). T010 explicitly depends on T013 ('Depends on T013'). A consumer task (test) cannot precede its producer (implementation) in the execution order. T013 must be moved before T010.

Task T014 (Adjust linear projection width) depends on T013 output, but T014 is listed after T011 (Implement dendritic model). The dendritic model (T011) must be implemented before its width can be adjusted (T014). The order should be T013 -> T011 -> T014.

Task T036 (Update transformer_dendritic.py docstrings) is listed after T035 (Create research_notes.md). While T035 is documentation, T036 modifies the core model code (T011). If T036 is intended to be a code update, it should logically follow the implementation of T011 in Phase 3, not be deferred to Phase 6 after analysis tasks. If it is purely documentation, it should be grouped with T040 (Polish) or explicitly marked as a post-implementation review task, but currently it breaks the flow of 'Implement -> Analyze -> Review' by placing a code-modification task after analysis tasks (T025-T034).

Task T015 (Add validation logic for activation function) is listed after T014. However, T015 validates the model defined in T011. While T014 adjusts the model, T015's validation logic is a property of the model implementation itself. It should ideally be part of the implementation block (T011) or immediately follow T011, before T014 (which depends on T013, not T015). The current order T013 -> T011 -> T014 -> T015 is acceptable but T015 feels out of place after the FLOP adjustment logic.
